problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON drift repair with explicit fail-closed handling for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function implements repair patterns for Claude JSON drift: line range format, empty key names, and trailing commas
evidence.why: This is the core logic that handles recoverable JSON formatting issues from Claude agents

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function attempts JSON extraction from direct text, fenced code blocks, and slice-by-braces fallback
evidence.why: Shows the multi-strategy approach to finding valid JSON in various agent output formats

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function returns explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, with missing array containing the error message
evidence.why: This implements the fail-closed requirement for unrecoverable JSON with explicit blocker codes

evidence.summary: Provider run result includes json_repaired boolean flag that tracks whether drift repair was applied
evidence.why: This is the recording mechanism for when minor drift is successfully repaired

evidence.summary: Test 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair' validates line format and empty key drift patterns are repaired and json_repaired flag is set
evidence.why: This test confirms the repair logic works for documented drift patterns

evidence.summary: Test 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' validates that non-JSON input returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON and keeps readiness blocked
evidence.why: This test confirms the fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required fields and contract for evidence agent results including error handling expectations
evidence.why: This defines the schema contract that agents must follow and what blockers are required

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine specifies that dispatch returns are fed back into PRD readiness, and any violation keeps blockers active
evidence.why: This is the product-level requirement that validates the implementation approach

assumptions: The implementation has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in production
Dirty working tree mentioned in status_quo may indicate development state but no evidence of pending changes
risks: If Claude introduces new JSON drift patterns beyond the three handled cases, agents may fail with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON even for recoverable issues
The repair logic is regex-based and may not handle all edge cases of malformed JSON
Test coverage may not include all possible Claude output variations

result.notes: Evidence confirms implementation exists with test coverage for JSON drift repair and fail-closed handling. The code structure matches the demand requirements: recoverable drift is repaired and recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness remains blocked until valid evidence is provided.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is verified: all claims have direct project-scoped evidence with usable paths, assumptions are properly separated, and missing data is correctly empty

evidence.summary: Explorer result JSON contains 8 evidence records with exact line numbers and repo-relative paths for all project-scoped claims
evidence.why: Confirms evidence traceability and that no assumption was promoted to fact

evidence.summary: Verified parseJsonCandidate, extractJsonObject, and normalizeAgentResult functions exist at claimed lines
evidence.why: Primary source code verification for JSON drift repair and fail-closed handling

evidence.summary: Verified test coverage exists for both drift repair and invalid JSON fail-closed scenarios
evidence.why: Confirms acceptance criteria have automated test validation

evidence.summary: Verified DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required evidence contract
evidence.why: Schema verification that evidence agents must follow explicit contracts

assumptions: Explorer correctly identified that the implementation has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in production
Explorer correctly identified missing data as empty since all required facts have project-scoped evidence
risks: Verification is limited to static file analysis; runtime behavior with real Claude agents cannot be confirmed without stress testing
Line numbers in evidence may become stale if files are modified

result.notes: Explorer evidence is well-structured: all 8 evidence records have exact paths and line numbers, assumptions are clearly separated from facts, and missing is correctly empty. The claim about JSON drift repair implementation is directly supported by project code and tests. No assumption was promoted as fact, and evidence paths are usable for verification.
